NKJV.1: A wise son heeds his father’s instruction, But a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
NKJV.2: A man shall eat well by the fruit of his mouth, But the soul of the unfaithful feeds on violence.
NKJV.3: He who guards his mouth preserves his life, But he who opens wide his lips shall have destruction.
NKJV.4: The soul of a lazy man desires, and has nothing; But the soul of the diligent shall be made rich.
NKJV.5: A righteous man hates lying, But a wicked man is loathsome and comes to shame.
NKJV.6: Righteousness guards him whose way is blameless, But wickedness overthrows the sinner.
NKJV.7: There is one who makes himself rich, yet has nothing; And one who makes himself poor, yet has great riches.
NKJV.8: The ransom of a man’s life is his riches, But the poor does not hear rebuke.
NKJV.9: The light of the righteous rejoices,  But the lamp of the wicked will be put out.
NKJV.10: By pride comes nothing but strife, But with the well-advised is wisdom.
NKJV.11: Wealth gained by dishonesty will be diminished, But he who gathers by labor will increase.
NKJV.12: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, But when the desire comes, it is a tree of life.
NKJV.13: He who despises the word will be destroyed, But he who fears the commandment will be rewarded.
NKJV.14: The law of the wise is a fountain of life, To turn one away from the snares of death.
NKJV.15: Good understanding gains favor, But the way of the unfaithful is hard.
NKJV.16: Every prudent man acts with knowledge, But a fool lays open his folly.
NKJV.17: A wicked messenger falls into trouble, But a faithful ambassador brings health.
NKJV.18: Poverty and shame will come to him who disdains correction, But he who regards a rebuke will be honored.
NKJV.19: A desire accomplished is sweet to the soul, But it is an abomination to fools to depart from evil.
NKJV.20: He who walks with wise men will be wise, But the companion of fools will be destroyed.
NKJV.21: Evil pursues sinners, But to the righteous, good shall be repaid.
NKJV.22: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children’s children, But the wealth of the sinner is stored up for the righteous.
NKJV.23: Much food is in the fallow ground of the poor, And for lack of justice there is waste.
NKJV.24: He who spares his rod hates his son, But he who loves him disciplines him promptly.
NKJV.25: The righteous eats to the satisfying of his soul, But the stomach of the wicked shall be in want.

ESV.1: A wise son hears his father's instruction, but a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
ESV.2: From the fruit of his mouth a man eats what is good, but the desire of the treacherous is for violence.
ESV.3: Whoever guards his mouth preserves his life; he who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.
ESV.4: The soul of the sluggard craves and gets nothing, while the soul of the diligent is richly supplied.
ESV.5: The righteous hates falsehood, but the wicked brings shame  and disgrace.
ESV.6: Righteousness guards him whose way is blameless, but sin overthrows the wicked.
ESV.7: One pretends to be rich,  yet has nothing; another pretends to be poor,  yet has great wealth.
ESV.8: The ransom of a man's life is his wealth, but a poor man hears no threat.
ESV.9: The light of the righteous rejoices, but the lamp of the wicked will be put out.
ESV.10: By insolence comes nothing but strife, but with those who take advice is wisdom.
ESV.11: Wealth gained hastily  will dwindle, but whoever gathers little by little will increase it.
ESV.12: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a desire fulfilled is a tree of life.
ESV.13: Whoever despises the word  brings destruction on himself, but he who reveres the commandment  will be rewarded.
ESV.14: The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, that one may turn away from the snares of death.
ESV.15: Good sense wins favor, but the way of the treacherous is their ruin.
ESV.16: Every prudent man acts with knowledge, but a fool flaunts his folly.
ESV.17: A wicked messenger falls into trouble, but a faithful envoy brings healing.
ESV.18: Poverty and disgrace come to him who ignores instruction, but whoever heeds reproof is honored.
ESV.19: A desire fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but to turn away from evil is an abomination to fools.
ESV.20: Whoever walks with the wise becomes wise, but the companion of fools will suffer harm.
ESV.21: Disaster  pursues sinners, but the righteous are rewarded with good.
ESV.22: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children, but the sinner's wealth is laid up for the righteous.
ESV.23: The fallow ground of the poor would yield much food, but it is swept away through injustice.
ESV.24: Whoever spares the rod hates his son, but he who loves him is diligent to discipline him.
ESV.25: The righteous has enough to satisfy his appetite, but the belly of the wicked suffers want.

NASB.1: A wise son [accepts his] father's discipline, But a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
NASB.2: From the fruit of a man's mouth he enjoys good, But the desire of the treacherous is violence.
NASB.3: The one who guards his mouth preserves his life; The one who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.
NASB.4: The soul of the sluggard craves and [gets] nothing, But the soul of the diligent is made fat.
NASB.5: A righteous man hates falsehood, But a wicked man acts disgustingly and shamefully.
NASB.6: Righteousness guards the one whose way is blameless, But wickedness subverts the sinner.
NASB.7: There is one who pretends to be rich, but has nothing; [Another] pretends to be poor, but has great wealth.
NASB.8: The ransom of a man's life is his wealth, But the poor hears no rebuke.
NASB.9: The light of the righteous rejoices, But the lamp of the wicked goes out.
NASB.10: Through insolence comes nothing but strife, But wisdom is with those who receive counsel.
NASB.11: Wealth [obtained] by fraud dwindles, But the one who gathers by labor increases [it].
NASB.12: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, But desire fulfilled is a tree of life.
NASB.13: The one who despises the word will be in debt to it, But the one who fears the commandment will be rewarded.
NASB.14: The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, To turn aside from the snares of death.
NASB.15: Good understanding produces favor, But the way of the treacherous is hard.
NASB.16: Every prudent man acts with knowledge, But a fool displays folly.
NASB.17: A wicked messenger falls into adversity, But a faithful envoy [brings] healing.
NASB.18: Poverty and shame [will come] to him who neglects discipline, But he who regards reproof will be honored.
NASB.19: Desire realized is sweet to the soul, But it is an abomination to fools to turn away from evil.
NASB.20: He who walks with wise men will be wise, But the companion of fools will suffer harm.
NASB.21: Adversity pursues sinners, But the righteous will be rewarded with prosperity.
NASB.22: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children, And the wealth of the sinner is stored up for the righteous.
NASB.23: Abundant food [is in] the fallow ground of the poor, But it is swept away by injustice.
NASB.24: He who withholds his rod hates his son, But he who loves him disciplines him diligently.
NASB.25: The righteous has enough to satisfy his appetite, But the stomach of the wicked is in need.

NLT.1:
A wise child accepts a parent’s discipline;
a mocker refuses to listen to correction.
NLT.2:
Wise words will win you a good meal,
but treacherous people have an appetite for violence.
NLT.3:
Those who control their tongue will have a long life;
opening your mouth can ruin everything.
NLT.4:
Lazy people want much but get little,
but those who work hard will prosper.
NLT.5:
The godly hate lies;
the wicked cause shame and disgrace.
NLT.6:
Godliness guards the path of the blameless,
but the evil are misled by sin.
NLT.7:
Some who are poor pretend to be rich;
others who are rich pretend to be poor.
NLT.8:
The rich can pay a ransom for their lives,
but the poor won’t even get threatened.
NLT.9:
The life of the godly is full of light and joy,
but the light of the wicked will be snuffed out.
NLT.10:
Pride leads to conflict;
those who take advice are wise.
NLT.11:
Wealth from get-rich-quick schemes quickly disappears;
wealth from hard work grows over time.
NLT.12:
Hope deferred makes the heart sick,
but a dream fulfilled is a tree of life.
NLT.13:
People who despise advice are asking for trouble;
those who respect a command will succeed.
NLT.14:
The instruction of the wise is like a life-giving fountain;
those who accept it avoid the snares of death.
NLT.15:
A person with good sense is respected;
a treacherous person is headed for destruction.
NLT.16:
Wise people think before they act;
fools don’t — and even brag about their foolishness.
NLT.17:
An unreliable messenger stumbles into trouble,
but a reliable messenger brings healing.
NLT.18:
If you ignore criticism, you will end in poverty and disgrace;
if you accept correction, you will be honored.
NLT.19:
It is pleasant to see dreams come true,
but fools refuse to turn from evil to attain them.
NLT.20:
Walk with the wise and become wise;
associate with fools and get in trouble.
NLT.21:
Trouble chases sinners,
while blessings reward the righteous.
NLT.22:
Good people leave an inheritance to their grandchildren,
but the sinner’s wealth passes to the godly.
NLT.23:
A poor person’s farm may produce much food,
but injustice sweeps it all away.
NLT.24:
Those who spare the rod of discipline hate their children.
Those who love their children care enough to discipline them.
NLT.25:
The godly eat to their hearts’ content,
but the belly of the wicked goes hungry.

WEB.1: A wise son listens to his father’s instruction, but a scoffer doesn’t listen to rebuke.
WEB.2: By the fruit of his lips, a man enjoys good things; but the unfaithful crave violence.
WEB.3: He who guards his mouth guards his soul. One who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.
WEB.4: The soul of the sluggard desires, and has nothing, but the desire of the diligent shall be fully satisfied.
WEB.5: A righteous man hates lies, but a wicked man brings shame and disgrace.
WEB.6: Righteousness guards the way of integrity, but wickedness overthrows the sinner.
WEB.7: There are some who pretend to be rich, yet have nothing. There are some who pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.
WEB.8: The ransom of a man’s life is his riches, but the poor hear no threats.
WEB.9: The light of the righteous shines brightly, but the lamp of the wicked is snuffed out.
WEB.10: Pride only breeds quarrels, but with ones who take advice is wisdom.
WEB.11: Wealth gained dishonestly dwindles away, but he who gathers by hand makes it grow.
WEB.12: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but when longing is fulfilled, it is a tree of life.
WEB.13: Whoever despises instruction will pay for it, but he who respects a command will be rewarded.
WEB.14: The teaching of the wise is a spring of life, to turn from the snares of death.
WEB.15: Good understanding wins favor; but the way of the unfaithful is hard.
WEB.16: Every prudent man acts from knowledge, but a fool exposes folly.
WEB.17: A wicked messenger falls into trouble, but a trustworthy envoy gains healing.
WEB.18: Poverty and shame come to him who refuses discipline, but he who heeds correction shall be honored.
WEB.19: Longing fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but fools detest turning from evil.
WEB.20: One who walks with wise men grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.
WEB.21: Misfortune pursues sinners, but prosperity rewards the righteous.
WEB.22: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children’s children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored for the righteous.
WEB.23: An abundance of food is in poor people’s fields, but injustice sweeps it away.
WEB.24: One who spares the rod hates his son, but one who loves him is careful to discipline him.
WEB.25: The righteous one eats to the satisfying of his soul, but the belly of the wicked goes hungry.

CSB17.1:
A wise son responds to his father’s discipline,
but a mocker doesn’t listen to rebuke.
CSB17.2:
From the fruit of his mouth,
a person will enjoy good things, ⓒ
but treacherous people have an appetite for violence. ⓓ
CSB17.3:
The one who guards his mouth protects his life; ⓔ
the one who opens his lips invites his own ruin. ⓕ
CSB17.4:
The slacker craves, yet has nothing,
but the diligent is fully satisfied. ⓖ
CSB17.5:
The righteous hate lying,
but the wicked bring disgust and shame.
CSB17.6:
Righteousness guards people of integrity, [1]
but wickedness undermines the sinner. ⓗ
CSB17.7:
One person pretends to be rich but has nothing; ⓘ
another pretends to be poor but has abundant wealth. ⓙ
CSB17.8:
Riches are a ransom for a person’s life, ⓚ
but a poor person hears no threat.
CSB17.9:
The light of the righteous shines brightly, ⓛ
but the lamp of the wicked is put out. ⓜ
CSB17.10:
Arrogance leads to nothing but strife, ⓝ
but wisdom is gained by those who take advice.
CSB17.11:
Wealth obtained by fraud will dwindle, ⓐ
but whoever earns it through labor [2] will multiply it. ⓑ
CSB17.12:
Hope delayed makes the heart sick,
but desire fulfilled is a tree of life. ⓒ
CSB17.13:
The one who has contempt for instruction will pay the penalty, ⓓ
but the one who respects a command will be rewarded. ⓔ
CSB17.14:
A wise person’s instruction is a fountain of life, ⓕ
turning people away from the snares of death. ⓖ
CSB17.15:
Good sense wins favor, ⓗ
but the way of the treacherous never changes. [3]
CSB17.16:
Every sensible person acts knowledgeably,
but a fool displays his stupidity. ⓘ
CSB17.17:
A wicked envoy falls into trouble,
but a trustworthy courier brings healing. ⓙ
CSB17.18:
Poverty and disgrace come to those
who ignore discipline,
but the one who accepts correction will be honored. ⓚ
CSB17.19:
Desire fulfilled is sweet to the taste,
but to turn from evil is detestable to fools. ⓛ
CSB17.20:
The one who walks with the wise will become wise,
but a companion of fools will suffer harm.
CSB17.21:
Disaster pursues sinners,
but good rewards the righteous. ⓜ
CSB17.22:
A good man leaves an inheritance to his [4] grandchildren,
but the sinner’s wealth is stored up for the righteous. ⓝ
CSB17.23:
The uncultivated field of the poor yields abundant food,
but without justice, it is swept away.
CSB17.24:
The one who will not use the rod hates his son, ⓐ
but the one who loves him disciplines him diligently. ⓑ
CSB17.25:
A righteous person eats until he is satisfied,
but the stomach of the wicked is empty. ⓒ

AMP.1:
A wise son heeds and accepts [and is the result of] his father's discipline and instruction,
But a scoffer does not listen to reprimand and does not learn from his errors.
AMP.2:
From the fruit of his mouth a [wise] man enjoys good,
But the desire of the treacherous is for violence.
AMP.3:
The one who guards his mouth [thinking before he speaks] protects his life;
The one who opens his lips wide [and chatters without thinking] comes to ruin.
AMP.4:
The soul (appetite) of the lazy person craves and gets nothing [for lethargy overcomes ambition],
But the soul (appetite) of the diligent [who works willingly] is rich and abundantly supplied.
AMP.5:
A righteous man hates lies,
But a wicked man is loathsome, and he acts shamefully.
AMP.6:
Righteousness (being in right standing with God) guards the one whose way is blameless,
But wickedness undermines and overthrows the sinner.
AMP.7:
There is one who pretends to be rich, yet has nothing at all;
Another pretends to be poor, yet has great wealth.
AMP.8:
The ransom for a man's life is his wealth,
But the poor man does not even have to listen to a rebuke or threats [from the envious].
AMP.9:
The light of the righteous [within him—grows brighter and] rejoices,
But the lamp of the wicked [is a temporary light and] goes out.
AMP.10:
Through pride and presumption come nothing but strife,
But [skillful and godly] wisdom is with those who welcome [well-advised] counsel.
AMP.11:
Wealth obtained by fraud dwindles,
But he who gathers gradually by [honest] labor will increase [his riches].
AMP.12:
Hope deferred makes the heart sick,
But when desire is fulfilled, it is a tree of life.
AMP.13:
Whoever despises the word and counsel [of God] brings destruction upon himself,
But he who [reverently] fears and respects the commandment [of God] will be rewarded.
AMP.14:
The teaching of the wise is a fountain and source of life,
So that one may avoid the snares of death.
AMP.15:
Good understanding wins favor [from others],
But the way of the unfaithful is hard [like barren, dry soil].
AMP.16:
Every prudent and self-disciplined man acts with knowledge,
But a [closed-minded] fool [who refuses to learn] displays his foolishness [for all to see].
AMP.17:
A wicked messenger falls into hardship,
But a faithful ambassador brings healing.
AMP.18:
Poverty and shame will come to him who refuses instruction and discipline,
But he who accepts and learns from reproof or censure is honored.
AMP.19:
Desire realized is sweet to the soul;
But it is detestable to fools to turn away from evil [which they have planned].
AMP.20:
He who walks [as a companion] with wise men will be wise,
But the companions of [conceited, dull-witted] fools [are fools themselves and] will experience harm.
AMP.21:
Adversity pursues sinners,
But the [consistently] upright will be rewarded with prosperity.
AMP.22:
A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children,
And the wealth of the sinner is stored up for [the hands of] the righteous.
AMP.23:
Abundant food is in the fallow (uncultivated) ground of the poor,
But [without protection] it is swept away by injustice.
AMP.24:
He who withholds the rod [of discipline] hates his son,
But he who loves him disciplines and trains him diligently and appropriately [with wisdom and love].
AMP.25:
The [consistently] righteous has enough to satisfy his appetite,
But the stomach of the wicked is in need [of bread].

MSG.1:
Intelligent children listen to their parents;
foolish children do their own thing.
MSG.2:
The good acquire a taste for helpful conversation;
bullies push and shove their way through life.
MSG.3:
Careful words make for a careful life;
careless talk may ruin everything.
MSG.4:
Indolence wants it all and gets nothing;
the energetic have something to show for their lives.
MSG.5:
A good person hates false talk;
a bad person wallows in gibberish.
MSG.6:
A God-loyal life keeps you on track;
sin dumps the wicked in the ditch.
MSG.7:
A pretentious, showy life is an empty life;
a plain and simple life is a full life.
MSG.8:
The rich can be sued for everything they have,
but the poor are free of such threats.
MSG.9:
The lives of good people are brightly lit streets;
the lives of the wicked are dark alleys.
MSG.10:
Arrogant know-it-alls stir up discord,
but wise men and women listen to each other's counsel.
MSG.11:
Easy come, easy go,
but steady diligence pays off.
MSG.12:
Unrelenting disappointment leaves you heartsick,
but a sudden good break can turn life around.
MSG.13:
Ignore the Word and suffer;
honor God's commands and grow rich.
MSG.14:
The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life,
so, no more drinking from death-tainted wells!
MSG.15:
Sound thinking makes for gracious living,
but liars walk a rough road.
MSG.16:
A commonsense person lives good sense;
fools litter the country with silliness.
MSG.17:
Irresponsible talk makes a real mess of things,
but a reliable reporter is a healing presence.
MSG.18:
Refuse discipline and end up homeless;
embrace correction and live an honored life.
MSG.19:
Souls who follow their hearts thrive;
fools bent on evil despise matters of soul.
MSG.20:
Become wise by walking with the wise;
hang out with fools and watch your life fall to pieces.
MSG.21:
Disaster entraps sinners,
but God-loyal people get a good life.
MSG.22:
A good life gets passed on to the grandchildren;
ill-gotten wealth ends up with good people.
MSG.23:
Banks foreclose on the farms of the poor,
or else the poor lose their shirts to crooked lawyers.
MSG.24:
A refusal to correct is a refusal to love;
love your children by disciplining them.
MSG.25:
An appetite for good brings much satisfaction,
but the belly of the wicked always wants more.

NIV.1:
A wise son heeds his father’s instruction,
but a mocker does not listen to rebuke.
NIV.2:
From the fruit of his lips a man enjoys good things,
but the unfaithful have a craving for violence.
NIV.3:
He who guards his lips guards his life,
but he who speaks rashly will come to ruin.
NIV.4:
The sluggard craves and gets nothing,
but the desires of the diligent are fully satisfied.
NIV.5:
The righteous hate what is false,
but the wicked bring shame and disgrace.
NIV.6:
Righteousness guards the man of integrity,
but wickedness overthrows the sinner.
NIV.7:
One man pretends to be rich, yet has nothing;
another pretends to be poor, yet has great wealth.
NIV.8:
A man’s riches may ransom his life,
but a poor man hears no threat.
NIV.9:
The light of the righteous shines brightly,
but the lamp of the wicked is snuffed out.
NIV.10:
Pride only breeds quarrels,
but wisdom is found in those who take advice.
NIV.11:
Dishonest money dwindles away,
but he who gathers money little by little makes it grow.
NIV.12:
Hope deferred makes the heart sick,
but a longing fulfilled is a tree of life.
NIV.13:
He who scorns instruction will pay for it,
but he who respects a command is rewarded.
NIV.14:
The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life,
turning a man from the snares of death.
NIV.15:
Good understanding wins favor,
but the way of the unfaithful is hard.
NIV.16:
Every prudent man acts out of knowledge,
but a fool exposes his folly.
NIV.17:
A wicked messenger falls into trouble,
but a trustworthy envoy brings healing.
NIV.18:
He who ignores discipline comes to poverty and shame,
but whoever heeds correction is honored.
NIV.19:
A longing fulfilled is sweet to the soul,
but fools detest turning from evil.
NIV.20:
He who walks with the wise grows wise,
but a companion of fools suffers harm.
NIV.21:
Misfortune pursues the sinner,
but prosperity is the reward of the righteous.
NIV.22:
A good man leaves an inheritance for his children’s children,
but a sinner’s wealth is stored up for the righteous.
NIV.23:
A poor man’s field may produce abundant food,
but injustice sweeps it away.
NIV.24:
He who spares the rod hates his son,
but he who loves him is careful to discipline him.
NIV.25:
The righteous eat to their hearts’ content,
but the stomach of the wicked goes hungry.

NRSVCE.1:
A wise child loves discipline,
but a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
NRSVCE.2:
From the fruit of their words good persons eat good things,
but the desire of the treacherous is for wrongdoing.
NRSVCE.3:
Those who guard their mouths preserve their lives;
those who open wide their lips come to ruin.
NRSVCE.4:
The appetite of the lazy craves, and gets nothing,
while the appetite of the diligent is richly supplied.
NRSVCE.5:
The righteous hate falsehood,
but the wicked act shamefully and disgracefully.
NRSVCE.6:
Righteousness guards one whose way is upright,
but sin overthrows the wicked.
NRSVCE.7:
Some pretend to be rich, yet have nothing;
others pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.
NRSVCE.8:
Wealth is a ransom for a person’s life,
but the poor get no threats.
NRSVCE.9:
The light of the righteous rejoices,
but the lamp of the wicked goes out.
NRSVCE.10:
By insolence the heedless make strife,
but wisdom is with those who take advice.
NRSVCE.11:
Wealth hastily gotten will dwindle,
but those who gather little by little will increase it.
NRSVCE.12:
Hope deferred makes the heart sick,
but a desire fulfilled is a tree of life.
NRSVCE.13:
Those who despise the word bring destruction on themselves,
but those who respect the commandment will be rewarded.
NRSVCE.14:
The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life,
so that one may avoid the snares of death.
NRSVCE.15:
Good sense wins favor,
but the way of the faithless is their ruin.
NRSVCE.16:
The clever do all things intelligently,
but the fool displays folly.
NRSVCE.17:
A bad messenger brings trouble,
but a faithful envoy, healing.
NRSVCE.18:
Poverty and disgrace are for the one who ignores instruction,
but one who heeds reproof is honored.
NRSVCE.19:
A desire realized is sweet to the soul,
but to turn away from evil is an abomination to fools.
NRSVCE.20:
Whoever walks with the wise becomes wise,
but the companion of fools suffers harm.
NRSVCE.21:
Misfortune pursues sinners,
but prosperity rewards the righteous.
NRSVCE.22:
The good leave an inheritance to their children’s children,
but the sinner’s wealth is laid up for the righteous.
NRSVCE.23:
The field of the poor may yield much food,
but it is swept away through injustice.
NRSVCE.24:
Those who spare the rod hate their children,
but those who love them are diligent to discipline them.
NRSVCE.25:
The righteous have enough to satisfy their appetite,
but the belly of the wicked is empty.

NET.1: A wise son accepts his father’s discipline, but a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
NET.2: From the fruit of his speech a person eats good things, but the faithless desire the fruit of violence.
NET.3: The one who guards his words guards his life, but whoever is talkative will come to ruin.
NET.4: The appetite of the sluggard craves but gets nothing, but the desire of the diligent will be abundantly satisfied.
NET.5: The righteous person hates anything false, but the wicked person acts in shameful disgrace.
NET.6: Righteousness guards the one who lives with integrity, but wickedness overthrows the sinner.
NET.7: There is one who pretends to be rich and yet has nothing; another pretends to be poor and yet possesses great wealth.
NET.8: The ransom of a person’s life is his wealth, but the poor person hears no threat.
NET.9: The light of the righteous shines brightly, but the lamp of the wicked goes out.
NET.10: With pride comes only contention, but wisdom is with the well-advised.
NET.11: Wealth gained quickly will dwindle away, but the one who gathers it little by little will become rich.
NET.12: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a longing fulfilled is like a tree of life.
NET.13: The one who despises instruction will pay the penalty, but whoever esteems instruction will be rewarded.
NET.14: Instruction from the wise is like a life-giving fountain, to turn a person from deadly snares.
NET.15: Keen insight wins favor, but the conduct of the unfaithful is harsh.
NET.16: Every shrewd person acts with knowledge, but a fool displays his folly.
NET.17: An unreliable messenger falls into trouble, but a faithful envoy brings healing.
NET.18: The one who neglects discipline ends up in poverty and shame, but the one who accepts reproof is honored.
NET.19: A desire fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but fools abhor turning away from evil.
NET.20: The one who associates with the wise grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.
NET.21: Calamity pursues sinners, but prosperity rewards the righteous.
NET.22: A benevolent person leaves an inheritance for his grandchildren, but the wealth of a sinner is stored up for the righteous.
NET.23: There is abundant food in the field of the poor, but it is swept away by injustice.
NET.24: The one who spares his rod hates his child, but the one who loves his child is diligent in disciplining him.
NET.25: The righteous has enough food to satisfy his appetite, but the belly of the wicked lacks food.
